checks.finding: All AC references in tasks.md resolve to AC-001 through AC-014 in acceptance-tests.md.
T-001 and T-002 declare None; T-003 through T-006 use comma-separated valid T-NNN identifiers. blockers_format_valid=true.
REQ-001 through REQ-012 are each referenced by at least one of T-001 through T-006.
T-002 covers AC-001, AC-002, AC-006, and AC-011; T-003 covers AC-003, AC-004, AC-005, AC-007, AC-008, and AC-014; T-004 covers AC-009; T-005 covers AC-010 and AC-012; T-006 covers AC-013.
Every task T-001 through T-006 references at least one REQ-NNN in its Requirements field.
No task title or scope defines an exclusively test/spec/verify task; test work is coupled to implementation concerns with explicit AC references where applicable.
In attempt 4 round 1, every task T-001 through T-006 has Approval: Draft and Status: Planned.
T-001 through T-005 pair high risk with tdd; T-006 pairs medium risk with acceptance-first. precheck-result.json also records workflow_match_precheck as PASS.
Each explicitly claimed AC is assigned once across T-002 through T-006; no duplicate primary AC target appears.
Every blocker reference resolves to a node in dependency-graph.json, whose nodes are exactly T-001 through T-006.
The dependency graph is acyclic: T-003 waits for T-001/T-002, T-004 and T-005 wait for T-003, and T-006 waits for T-004/T-005.
Each task has one coherent concern: transition repair, registry/migration, validator implementation, repository/CI integration, gate integration, or release metadata.

Every task names inspectable commands, binary validation outcomes, or evidence artifacts; T-001 through T-005 name verification logs and implementation reports, while T-006 names version assertions, repository validation, and its implementation report.
traceability.md contains Task Mapping entries for every task T-001 through T-006, and its requirement table references only defined identifiers REQ-001 through REQ-012.
